plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that core clinical traits already align with innovation leadership traits, reducing perceived identity distance between physician and innovator.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing innovation as foreign to seeing it as an extension of physicianship through trait overlap and professional reframing
plan_units.rationale: This opener establishes the section thesis and reframes innovation as an extension of existing physicianship traits. It is central but works best as a simultaneous whole rather than decomposition.
source_refs: You do not need to learn
plan_units.title: Physicianship = Innovation Leadership

plan_units.learning_objective: Map the clinical method to design thinking so learners can transfer familiar diagnostic reasoning into innovation work.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as a familiar clinical reasoning loop through direct workflow mapping
plan_units.rationale: This is a high-pedagogical-weight bridge concept with multiple explicit mappings. It benefits from progressive disclosure: establish the parallel, surface the workflow shift, then land the transfer claim.
plan_units.title: The Bridge: Diagnosis = Design Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain why taking a patient's history corresponds to the empathize stage of design thinking.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as a familiar clinical reasoning loop through direct workflow mapping
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial isolates the first transfer step so learners can anchor design thinking in a familiar clinical act before moving to the rest of the framework.
source_refs: When you take a patient's history
the first step of Design Thinking
plan_units.title: Clinical History Maps to Empathize

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain how diagnosis, differential building, and treatment planning correspond to define, ideate, and prototype in design thinking.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as a familiar clinical reasoning loop through direct workflow mapping
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial completes the transfer mapping and explicitly shifts the object of care from a patient to a workflow or system.
source_refs: synthesize the physical exam and labs
changing the 'patient' from a human being to a broken workflow or system
plan_units.title: Diagnosis and Treatment Map to Define, Ideate, Prototype

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify intrapreneurship as a structured, replicable formula that requires balancing clinical vision with organizational realities.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating intrapreneurship as a vague personality trait to understanding it as a structured internal change formula through guided reading and framing
plan_units.rationale: The reading block is explicitly required in the source and introduces a major conceptual scaffold for the remaining section. It can remain dense because its purpose is framing and assigning focus for deeper reading.
source_refs: balance your clinical vision with the realities of your organizational environment
plan_units.title: Required Reading: Sandra Lam’s Intrapreneurship Formula

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship and define the clinician’s primary arena for innovation as change within an existing health system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startups to locating clinician innovation within existing institutions through direct contrast
plan_units.rationale: This comparison is conceptually central and the source specifies a split-screen definition format. Its value lies in the simultaneous contrast, so it should remain a keep-dense singleton.
source_refs: We often conflate innovation with Entrepreneurship
The most immediate impact you can make is through Intrapreneurship
improve it from the inside out
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship because change must occur within legacy, compliant, budgeted institutional structures.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming all innovation means starting fresh to understanding internal hospital innovation as constrained redesign through a blank-canvas versus blueprint contrast
plan_units.rationale: This metaphor carries several explanatory beats and is pedagogically important because it makes internal constraints tangible. It benefits from a full arc: contrast, complication, then operational takeaway.
source_refs: without destroying the existing, load-bearing institutional structures
plan_units.title: The Intrapreneur’s Maze: Building Inside Load-Bearing Structures

plan_units.learning_objective: Describe entrepreneurship as building from scratch in a comparatively unconstrained environment.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming all innovation means starting fresh to understanding internal hospital innovation as constrained redesign through a blank-canvas versus blueprint contrast
plan_units.rationale: This tension beat sharpens the contrast by first showing the relative freedom of entrepreneurship.
source_refs: building a small house from scratch
plan_units.title: Entrepreneur: The Blank Canvas

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify the key institutional constraints—legacy IT, compliance, politics, budgets—that shape intrapreneurial action.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming all innovation means starting fresh to understanding internal hospital innovation as constrained redesign through a blank-canvas versus blueprint contrast
plan_units.rationale: This develop beat surfaces the actual constraints clinicians must navigate inside the health system.
source_refs: inside a highly complex, glowing neon-blue architectural blueprint
plan_units.title: Intrapreneur: Remodeling the Hospital Blueprint

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply the metaphor to the course frame: innovation inside hospitals requires systems literacy and careful redesign rather than disruptive destruction.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming all innovation means starting fresh to understanding internal hospital innovation as constrained redesign through a blank-canvas versus blueprint contrast
plan_units.rationale: This resolve beat converts the metaphor into the operating stance of the course.
source_refs: you will bring the roof down
redesign the room without breaking the building
legacy IT systems, compliance rules, and operational budgets
plan_units.title: Redesign the Room Without Breaking the Building

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate a low-value idea from a vetted opportunity with a clear path to execution inside a specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual solution generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through definition, vetting criteria, and organizational fit
plan_units.rationale: This is one of the section’s most important threshold concepts. Because it contains multiple definitional and evaluative beats, it benefits from chunking into definition, criteria, and applied reframing.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought
rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
plan_units.title: Idea vs. Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that jumping to a solution without validation does not create organizational value.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual solution generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through definition, vetting criteria, and organizational fit
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial isolates the course’s strict stance that ideas are insufficient on their own.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought or a suggestion
We should use AI to write notes
plan_units.title: Why an Idea Alone Has Zero Value

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain that an opportunity is an idea vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability within the learner’s specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual solution generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through definition, vetting criteria, and organizational fit
plan_units.rationale: This resolve beat lands the operational standard that the program expects learners to use.
plan_units.title: Engineering an Actionable Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain first-principles thinking as breaking the status quo into fundamental truths before rebuilding a better system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching inherited workflows to rebuilding from fundamental truths through deconstruction and reassembly
plan_units.rationale: This visual metaphor is dense but coherent as a before-middle-after progression. Its value is the whole transformation sequence, so it should stay as a keep-dense singleton.
source_refs: Do not just iterate on "how we have always done it."
Building a new system from the ground up
plan_units.title: First Principles Thinking: The Deconstruction

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply rigorous problem framing to rework a superficial technology solution into a root-cause-based opportunity statement.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-jumping to root-cause opportunity framing through critique, diagnosis, and reframed problem definition
plan_units.rationale: This embedded example is explicitly intended as a worked example and supports application-level transfer. It benefits from a full cluster that walks learners from superficial complaint to root-cause framing.
plan_units.title: Worked Example: From Bad Idea to Good Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify a superficial problem statement that jumps prematurely to a technology solution.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-jumping to root-cause opportunity framing through critique, diagnosis, and reframed problem definition
plan_units.rationale: This tension beat shows the common failure mode learners must avoid.
source_refs: My idea is to buy iPads for all the nurses
It assumes technology fixes human workflows
plan_units.title: The Bad Start: Buying iPads

plan_units.learning_objective: Analyze the underlying workflow cause behind delayed discharge rather than treating the visible symptom.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-jumping to root-cause opportunity framing through critique, diagnosis, and reframed problem definition
plan_units.rationale: This develop beat shifts learners from complaint to diagnosis using the scenario’s specific operational details.
source_refs: Discharge times are delayed by an average of 3 hours
do not have a standardized, easily accessible checklist
causing redundant trips to the nursing station
plan_units.title: Root-Cause Analysis of Delayed Discharge

plan_units.learning_objective: Construct an opportunity statement that links root cause, workflow redesign, and operational value.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-jumping to root-cause opportunity framing through critique, diagnosis, and reframed problem definition
plan_units.rationale: This resolve beat models the target form learners should emulate in later discussion work.
plan_units.title: The Good Reframing: A Standardized Mobile-Friendly Workflow

plan_units.learning_objective: Synthesize the section’s three core takeaways and prepare learners for the transition from problem framing to leadership identity in Module 2.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From discrete concepts to an integrated clinician-innovator frame through concise synthesis and forward transition
plan_units.rationale: This is a summary/transition slide whose value is the simultaneous recap and forward bridge. It should remain a dense singleton.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

plan_units.learning_objective: Verify recall and understanding of the section’s key distinctions: idea vs. opportunity, clinical method vs. design thinking, intrapreneurship, and problem attachment.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From exposure to verified comprehension through targeted recall and understanding checks
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly includes a five-question knowledge check. It should remain a single assessment unit rather than being decomposed into separate content clusters.
source_refs: fall in love with the problem
plan_units.title: Part 3 Knowledge Check

research_goals.pedagogical_intent: Learner needs the primary-source basis for Sandra Lam’s intrapreneurship formula framing and terminology used in the reading companion.
Learner would benefit from authoritative examples of design-thinking language applied in healthcare workflow redesign to reinforce the clinical-method crosswalk.
Learner needs concrete institutional examples of compliance, budgeting, and legacy-IT constraints in health-system innovation to deepen the intrapreneurship metaphor.

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A fuller written crosswalk explaining each paired step, why History aligns to Empathize, why Diagnosis aligns to Define, and how differential-building and treatment selection function like ideation and prototyping in operational redesign.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The side-by-side process map and the high-level transfer claim.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: When you take a patient's history
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners explain the correspondence between a clinical step and a design-thinking step in their own words.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: changing the 'patient' from a human being to a broken workflow or system
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to map a broken workflow to the same design-thinking sequence used in patient care.

sections.narrative_intent: Give clinicians enough explanatory depth to internalize design thinking as familiar rather than foreign, so the slide narration can stay concise.
sections.title: Clinical Method to Design Thinking Crosswalk

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A guided reading companion that helps learners extract the formula’s structured elements, especially balancing vision with organizational environment and stakeholder alignment.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The assignment cue and the formula’s role in the course arc.

exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to summarize how the reading turns intrapreneurship into a repeatable method.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: master the art of internal stakeholder alignment
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners identify which internal constraints in their own organization would most affect scaling a solution.

sections.narrative_intent: Shift deeper conceptual unpacking into the workbook so the slide remains a frame-setting required-reading block.
sections.title: Reading Companion: Sandra Lam’s Intrapreneurship Formula

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A worksheet for listing local examples of legacy IT, compliance, hospital politics, and operating budgets, plus notes on how each can constrain or enable an initiative.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The core blank-canvas versus load-bearing-blueprint metaphor.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners sort real barriers from their institution into the four constraint categories used in the visual.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: redesign the room without breaking the building
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to describe how they would redesign one room without breaking the building in a current workflow problem.

sections.narrative_intent: Make the metaphor operational by helping learners convert abstract institutional friction into named design constraints.
sections.title: Constraint Mapping for the Intrapreneur’s Maze

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A structured template for testing a proposed solution against feasibility, viability, desirability, and organizational path-to-execution criteria.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The stark distinction between idea and opportunity.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners classify statements as idea or opportunity and justify why.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
exercises.prompt_intent: Guide learners to transform one raw idea from their environment into an opportunity statement using the course criteria.

sections.narrative_intent: Carry the evaluative rigor off-slide so learners can practice vetting without overloading the deck.
sections.title: Idea-to-Opportunity Vetting Worksheet

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A deeper prose explanation contrasting reasoning by analogy with first-principles thinking, including prompts to identify the 'raw blocks' of a current workflow.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The three-phase deconstruction visual and labels.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: First Principles (Breaking it down to fundamental truths)
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to break one inherited workflow into fundamental truths rather than inherited conventions.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Building a new system from the ground up
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners sketch a rebuilt workflow from the same raw components.

sections.narrative_intent: Preserve the slide as a memorable visual metaphor while moving the reflective and generative work into the workbook.
sections.title: First-Principles Deconstruction Notes

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A fully expanded walkthrough showing the bad formulation, why it fails, how root-cause analysis proceeds, and how the final opportunity statement is constructed.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The headline contrast between the bad idea and the good reframing.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners diagnose why a solution-first statement is weak problem framing.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: The Opportunity: To design a standardized, mobile-friendly discharge workflow
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to write their own chief complaint, root cause, and opportunity statement from a local workflow issue.

sections.narrative_intent: Use the workbook to slow down the reasoning sequence learners will later need for graded discussion work.
sections.title: Worked Example Expansion: From Complaint to Opportunity

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Brief answer rationales and reflection prompts explaining why each correct answer is correct and what misconception each distractor represents.

exercises.prompt_intent: Support recall of the section’s key definitions and mappings.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: fall in love with the problem
exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to explain one misconception they held before taking the check.

sections.narrative_intent: Keep the deck assessment brisk while giving learners a durable post-check review surface.
sections.title: Knowledge Check Review and Reflection

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: Part 3 reframes clinicians as natural innovators, maps clinical reasoning onto design thinking, distinguishes intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship, and moves learners from superficial ideas toward vetted opportunities using first-principles and worked-example problem framing. Clustered treatment is applied to the highest-density threshold concepts and examples, while comparisons, metaphors that work as gestalts, summary, and assessment remain keep-dense singletons.

plan_units.rationale: This develop beat surfaces the actual constraints clinicians must navigate inside the health system.

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply the metaphor to the course frame: innovation inside hospitals requires systems literacy and careful redesign rather than disruptive destruction.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming all innovation means starting fresh to understanding internal hospital innovation as constrained redesign through a blank-canvas versus blueprint contrast
plan_units.rationale: This resolve beat converts the metaphor into the operating stance of the course.

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate a low-value idea from a vetted opportunity with a clear path to execution inside a specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual solution generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through definition, vetting criteria, and organizational fit
plan_units.rationale: This is one of the section’s most important threshold concepts. Because it contains multiple definitional and evaluative beats, it benefits from chunking into definition, criteria, and applied reframing.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought
rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
plan_units.title: Idea vs. Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that jumping to a solution without validation does not create organizational value.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual solution generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through definition, vetting criteria, and organizational fit
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial isolates the course’s strict stance that ideas are insufficient on their own.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought or a suggestion
We should use AI to write notes

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain that an opportunity is an idea vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability within the learner’s specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual solution generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through definition, vetting criteria, and organizational fit
plan_units.rationale: This resolve beat lands the operational standard that the program expects learners to use.
plan_units.title: Engineering an Actionable Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain first-principles thinking as breaking the status quo into fundamental truths before rebuilding a better system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching inherited workflows to rebuilding from fundamental truths through deconstruction and reassembly
plan_units.rationale: This visual metaphor is dense but coherent as a before-middle-after progression. Its value is the whole transformation sequence, so it should stay as a keep-dense singleton.
source_refs: Do not just iterate on "how we have always done it."
Building a new system from the ground up
plan_units.title: First Principles Thinking: The Deconstruction

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply rigorous problem framing to rework a superficial technology solution into a root-cause-based opportunity statement.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-jumping to root-cause opportunity framing through critique, diagnosis, and reframed problem definition
plan_units.rationale: This embedded example is explicitly intended as a worked example and supports application-level transfer. It benefits from a full cluster that walks learners from superficial complaint to root-cause framing.
plan_units.title: Worked Example: From Bad Idea to Good Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify a superficial problem statement that jumps prematurely to a technology solution.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-jumping to root-cause opportunity framing through critique, diagnosis, and reframed problem definition
plan_units.rationale: This tension beat shows the common failure mode learners must avoid.
source_refs: My idea is to buy iPads for all the nurses
It assumes technology fixes human workflows
plan_units.title: The Bad Start: Buying iPads

plan_units.learning_objective: Analyze the underlying workflow cause behind delayed discharge rather than treating the visible symptom.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-jumping to root-cause opportunity framing through critique, diagnosis, and reframed problem definition
plan_units.rationale: This develop beat shifts learners from complaint to diagnosis using the scenario’s specific operational details.

do not have a standardized, easily accessible checklist
causing redundant trips to the nursing station

plan_units.learning_objective: Construct an opportunity statement that links root cause, workflow redesign, and operational value.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-jumping to root-cause opportunity framing through critique, diagnosis, and reframed problem definition
plan_units.rationale: This resolve beat models the target form learners should emulate in later discussion work.
plan_units.title: The Good Reframing: A Standardized Mobile-Friendly Workflow

plan_units.learning_objective: Synthesize the section’s three core takeaways and prepare learners for the transition from problem framing to leadership identity in Module 2.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From discrete concepts to an integrated clinician-innovator frame through concise synthesis and forward transition
plan_units.rationale: This is a summary/transition slide whose value is the simultaneous recap and forward bridge. It should remain a dense singleton.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

plan_units.learning_objective: Verify recall and understanding of the section’s key distinctions: idea vs. opportunity, clinical method vs. design thinking, intrapreneurship, and problem attachment.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From exposure to verified comprehension through targeted recall and understanding checks
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly includes a five-question knowledge check. It should remain a single assessment unit rather than being decomposed into separate content clusters.
source_refs: fall in love with the problem
plan_units.title: Part 3 Knowledge Check

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that core clinical traits already align with innovation leadership traits, reducing perceived identity distance between physician and innovator.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing innovation as foreign to seeing it as an extension of physicianship through trait overlap and professional reframing
plan_units.rationale: This opener establishes the section thesis and reframes innovation as an extension of existing physicianship traits. It is central but works best as a simultaneous whole rather than decomposition.
source_refs: You do not need to learn
plan_units.title: Physicianship = Innovation Leadership

plan_units.learning_objective: Map the clinical method to design thinking so learners can transfer familiar diagnostic reasoning into innovation work.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as a familiar clinical reasoning loop through direct workflow mapping
plan_units.rationale: This is a high-pedagogical-weight bridge concept with multiple explicit mappings. It benefits from progressive disclosure: establish the parallel, surface the workflow shift, then land the transfer claim.
plan_units.title: The Bridge: Diagnosis = Design Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain why taking a patient's history corresponds to the empathize stage of design thinking.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as a familiar clinical reasoning loop through direct workflow mapping
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial isolates the first transfer step so learners can anchor design thinking in a familiar clinical act before moving to the rest of the framework.
source_refs: When you take a patient's history
the first step of Design Thinking
plan_units.title: Clinical History Maps to Empathize

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain how diagnosis, differential building, and treatment planning correspond to define, ideate, and prototype in design thinking.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as a familiar clinical reasoning loop through direct workflow mapping
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial completes the transfer mapping and explicitly shifts the object of care from a patient to a workflow or system.
source_refs: synthesize the physical exam and labs
changing the 'patient' from a human being to a broken workflow or system
plan_units.title: Diagnosis and Treatment Map to Define, Ideate, Prototype

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify intrapreneurship as a structured, replicable formula that requires balancing clinical vision with organizational realities.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating intrapreneurship as a vague personality trait to understanding it as a structured internal change formula through guided reading and framing
plan_units.rationale: The reading block is explicitly required in the source and introduces a major conceptual scaffold for the remaining section. It can remain dense because its purpose is framing and assigning focus for deeper reading.
source_refs: balance your clinical vision with the realities of your organizational environment
plan_units.title: Required Reading: Sandra Lam’s Intrapreneurship Formula

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship and define the clinician’s primary arena for innovation as change within an existing health system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startups to locating clinician innovation within existing institutions through direct contrast
plan_units.rationale: This comparison is conceptually central and the source specifies a split-screen definition format. Its value lies in the simultaneous contrast, so it should remain a keep-dense singleton.
source_refs: We often conflate innovation with Entrepreneurship
The most immediate impact you can make is through Intrapreneurship
improve it from the inside out
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship because change must occur within legacy, compliant, budgeted institutional structures.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming all innovation means starting fresh to understanding internal hospital innovation as constrained redesign through a blank-canvas versus blueprint contrast
plan_units.rationale: This metaphor carries several explanatory beats and is pedagogically important because it makes internal constraints tangible. It benefits from a full arc: contrast, complication, then operational takeaway.
source_refs: without destroying the existing, load-bearing institutional structures
plan_units.title: The Intrapreneur’s Maze: Building Inside Load-Bearing Structures

plan_units.learning_objective: Describe entrepreneurship as building from scratch in a comparatively unconstrained environment.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming all innovation means starting fresh to understanding internal hospital innovation as constrained redesign through a blank-canvas versus blueprint contrast
plan_units.rationale: This tension beat sharpens the contrast by first showing the relative freedom of entrepreneurship.
source_refs: building a small house from scratch
